Test Description: Register default-value verification and write-read validation across GPIO GP0 per-pin registers (GPIO_8..GPIO_39) and GPIO group I/O, DOUT, DIN, and interrupt registers using provided read/write masks and skip lists.
Remarks:
- Default-value check masks off bit0 (0xFFFFFFFE) before compare.
- VRRW-class and reset-skipped registers are omitted per skip arrays.
- Note: comment indicates DIN may read as 1 by default; forcing 0 can affect bit-level select, causing mismatch.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Setup
- Initialize failure counters for default-value and write-read mismatches to zero.
- Load arrays: register addresses, expected defaults, read masks, write masks, skip controls.

Default-value verification (for all indices 0..CNT-1)
- Select target register address from the address array.
- If reset-skip is asserted for the index, skip default compare.
- If read mask equals 0x00000000, treat as non-readable and skip.
- Read register; AND with 0xFFFFFFFE; compare against expected default value for the index.
- On mismatch, increment default-fail counter and record the address and values.

Patterned write/readback (for patterns: FFFFFFFF, AAAAAAAA, 55555555, F5F5F5F5, A5A5A5A5, FFFF0000)
- For each address index:
  - If skip is asserted, skip write/read for this address.
  - If write mask is 0x00000000, skip write; otherwise write (pattern AND write mask) to the address.
- For each address index:
  - If skipped, or write mask is 0x00000000, or read mask is 0x00000000, skip compare.
  - Read register; AND with read mask.
  - Compute expected: (pattern AND read mask AND write mask) OR ((bitwise NOT of write mask) AND read mask AND default value).
  - If readback differs from expected, increment write-fail counter and log address and values.

Finish
- If any failure counter is non-zero, mark test failure; otherwise mark success.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- All readable registers match expected defaults after masking bit0.
- For each pattern and each address with read/write capability, readback equals the computed expected value.
- Overall pass if both failure counters remain zero; otherwise fail.
Gap Analysis:
- LSB behavior (bit0) deliberately excluded from default check.
- VRRW and reset-skipped addresses are not exercised.
- No dedicated negative tests beyond mismatch detection.

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and servicing across GPIO[8..39]; includes system interrupt enable, per-pin configuration, raw status clear, group status verification, and system/GIC clears.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and system enables
- If built with GPIO0: enable IRQ 87 and enable GPIO0 interrupt in system interrupt enable register.
- If built with GPIO1: enable IRQ 88 and enable GPIO1 interrupt in system interrupt enable register.

Per-pin configuration (for indices 0..31 mapping to GPIO[8..39])
- For each per-pin register at base plus (index * 4): set bits 20, 18, and 16 to configure acknowledge/clear and interrupt mode per block definition; apply a brief wait.

Per-pin stimulus and wait (for indices 0..31)
- Compute wr_val as a one-hot for the current index.
- Clear any pending raw status by writing wr_val to the raw status clear register (write-one-to-clear semantics).
- Enable the corresponding group interrupt bit by writing wr_val to the group enable register; brief wait.
- Arm the test by setting the interrupt-pending flag.
- Drive pad control at 0xA0243FFC to all ones; wait; then drive to bitwise NOT of wr_val, creating a high-to-low transition on the selected pad.
- Poll the interrupt-pending flag with a timeout of 5000 iterations, waiting briefly each time; on timeout, log an error for this pad.

Interrupt service routine
- On entry, clear the interrupt-pending flag.
- Read the per-pin register for the current index; if the level bit indicates high, record an error for expected low after a negedge.
- If the per-pin status bit indicates an interrupt cause:
  - Read group interrupt status and verify the corresponding one-hot bit is set; else record error.
  - Acknowledge per-pin by writing bits 20 and 16 to the per-pin register.
  - Clear raw group status by writing the one-hot to the raw status clear register.
  - Read group status again and expect 0x0; else record error.
- Clear system-level raw status for the active GPIO instance and clear the GIC IRQ (87 for GPIO0, 88 for GPIO1).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout while polling for ISR on any index 0..31.
- In ISR: per-pin level indicates expected low after negedge; group status shows the one-hot bit set on entry and becomes 0 after clears.
- System-level raw status is cleared; GIC IRQ is cleared per instance.
- Overall pass if test_err remains zero at finish; otherwise fail.
Gap Analysis:
- No explicit negative/error-path tests beyond timeout.
- Does not validate DIN group reads.
- Enable mask remains active during ISR except for explicit masking in service (not present here).

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable and servicing across GPIO[8..39]; includes system/group interrupt configuration, I/O control setup, per-pin raw clear, group status deassertion, and system/GIC clears.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Environment and system enables
- If built with GPIO0: enable IRQ 87 and enable GPIO0 interrupt in system interrupt enable register.
- If built with GPIO1: enable IRQ 88 and enable GPIO1 interrupt in system interrupt enable register.

Per-pin posedge configuration
- For indices 0..31: write 0x00020000 to per-pin registers to enable posedge detection (bit17=1); brief wait.

I/O control configuration
- Write 0x000000FF to each I/O control group register 1..4 to configure pads; brief wait.

Group interrupt enable
- Write 0xFFFFFFFF to the group interrupt enable register.

Per-pin stimulus and wait (for indices 0..31)
- Drive pad control at 0xA0243FFC low (0x00000000); brief wait.
- Arm by setting the interrupt-pending flag to 1 before generating the edge.
- Drive pad control high (0xFFFFFFFF) to create a rising edge.
- Poll the interrupt-pending flag with a timeout of 2000 iterations; on timeout, log error and break.
- Drive low again and wait to prepare for the next index.

Interrupt service routine
- Clear the interrupt-pending flag and compute one-hot for the current index.
- Read group status; temporarily mask group output by writing 0 to the group enable during service.
- If any bit set, treat as valid entry; otherwise record error.
- Clear per-pin raw status by writing 0x00010000 to each per-pin register (j=0..31); brief wait.
- Read group status; expect 0x0; else record error.
- Clear system-level raw status for the active instance and verify the bit is cleared by readback; re-enable group enable for next iteration; clear the GIC IRQ (87/88).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout while polling for ISR on any index 0..31.
- Group interrupt status asserted on ISR entry and deasserted to 0x0 after per-pin and group clears.
- System-level raw status bit cleared by write and confirmed by readback; GIC IRQ cleared.
- Overall pass if test_err remains zero at finish; otherwise fail.
Gap Analysis:
- No explicit negative/error-path tests beyond timeout.
- Per-pin status not inspected individually after bulk clear.
- Debounce/noise behavior not exercised.
